rationale: The signal set is heavily weighted toward general crested gecko queries (care, diet, enclosure, morphs) with strong search volume but limited breeding-specific controversy or misconception. Google autocomplete shows sustained demand for "crested gecko morph chart" (+140%), "morph identification," and "breeding weight/age," while YouTube reveals a heated debate around Cappuccino viability (see "Don't buy super cappuccino... it's a scam") and Lilly White breeding strategy. Bluesky engagement is mostly lifestyle/art posts with no substantive breeding discussion. The single breeder blog is promotional. I found no Reddit threads, which limits community friction signals. I'm rejecting generic morph guides (already published: "how-to-identify-crested-gecko-morphs", "cappuccino-crested-gecko-genetics") and care content (already published). Instead, I'm targeting one high-demand, high-controversy topic with clear breeder-to-breeder disagreement: why Frappuccino outcomes confuse breeders, with a misconception-correction angle that aligns with voice fit.
